title: ⚠ MAP KEY — these are MODERN political borders
description: All coloured regions are MODERN country borders. The Andean coca homeland pre-dates modern Colombia + Peru + Bolivia by millennia — Quechua and Aymara peoples lived across these highlands long before the Spanish viceroyalties or post-independence nation-states existed.

Bolivia (modern borders) — traditional + legal coca heartland
description: Modern political border. Evo Morales' 2009 constitution made coca chewing explicitly legal. Bolivia successfully negotiated a UN reservation in 2013 removing coca chewing from the 1961 Single Convention's prohibition.

Peru (modern borders) — VRAEM + Huallaga illicit cultivation
description: Modern political border. VRAEM (Valley of the Apurímac, Ene, Mantaro rivers) + Huallaga valley produce most illicit coca. Traditional chewing remains culturally legal but production for cocaine is criminalized.

Colombia (modern borders) — world's largest cocaine supplier
description: Modern political border. Catatumbo, Nariño, Cauca, Putumayo anchor production. Despite Plan Colombia + $10B+ US aid + aerial fumigation, Colombia remains the top global supplier. 2016 FARC peace deal changed armed-conflict landscape but not production.

USA (modern borders) — world's largest consumer market
description: Modern political border. US is the #1 cocaine consumer market. Miami is the primary entry via Caribbean + Central American routes; Los Angeles via Mexico. Disproportionate Drug War enforcement has devastated Black + Latino communities (Alexander 'The New Jim Crow').

Europe (modern borders) — growing consumer market
description: Modern political borders. European cocaine market has grown massively since 2015; Antwerp + Rotterdam container ports are main entry points. Netherlands + Belgium report record cocaine seizures year after year.
